reasons: claudecode is the ONLY agent that provides executable code artifact (screener.py) with complete implementation of all required features
The screener.py demonstrates proper dual-timeframe analysis: separate daily and weekly MACD calculations with correct lookback periods (10 days for daily, 4 weeks for weekly)
Code includes all 4 required conditions: daily MACD golden cross, weekly MACD golden cross, MA5 positive slope check (monotonically increasing), and volume ratio > 1.2
Proper weekly data conversion from daily data using resample('W-FRI') method, showing understanding of timeframe aggregation
Complete MACD calculation implementation with EMA-based DIFF and DEA, proper golden cross detection logic
Output file satisfies contract (dual_timeframe_macd.txt exists with correct format and headers)
While ga found 2 stocks vs claudecode's 'no stocks found', ga provides NO code or methodology verification - results cannot be validated or reproduced
codex also found no stocks but provides no executable code, making it inferior to claudecode
openclaw completely failed - missing the required output file entirely
claudecode's executable artifact provides transparency, reproducibility, and verifiability - critical for technical analysis tasks
merge_notes: claudecode provides the most complete and professional solution with executable code that implements all technical requirements. The presence of screener.py with proper dual-timeframe MACD analysis, weekly data conversion, slope checking, and volume ratio calculation demonstrates superior technical implementation. While ga claims to have found 2 stocks, without code or methodology, those results cannot be verified or trusted. In quantitative finance tasks, reproducibility and verifiable methodology are paramount - claudecode is the only agent that satisfies this requirement.
